meta.currency_note: USD primary, INR converted at 85 (aligned with v2 dashboard capture 2026-04-20)
meta.disclaimer: Spend figures and protocols compiled from publicly available interviews, books, podcasts, documentaries, and reporting. Linked sources are the primary records we could verify directly. Sources shown as "public record" are well-attested in press and biographies but lack a single canonical URL. Items marked estimated are inferred from reported habits and industry pricing — not disclosed directly by the subject. ER scores for public figures are editorial estimates, not quiz-derived.

profiles.name: Bryan Johnson
profiles.tagline: The open-source biohack. $2M a year, 67 supplements a day, biological age of 18.
profiles.bio: Founder who sold Braintree/Venmo to PayPal for $800M, then turned his own body into a research site. His Blueprint protocol is fully open-sourced with every biomarker published — an N=1 experiment running in public since 2021.

spend_breakdown.label: Diagnostics, MRI, blood panels, DEXA
spend_breakdown.note: Quarterly full-body MRI, monthly blood panels across 70+ markers

spend_breakdown.label: Supplements & compounds
spend_breakdown.note: 67 daily pills, NAD+ drips, peptides, rapamycin

spend_breakdown.label: Therapies — red light, plasma, HBOT
spend_breakdown.note: Daily red light, weekly plasma, HBOT 5x/week

spend_breakdown.label: Equipment & home facility
spend_breakdown.note: Red light bed, hyperbaric chamber, cryotherapy unit

profiles.er_low_because: Sleep is gospel (8.5h nightly, 67°F room, no food 6h before bed), diet is clean calorie-restriction, movement is daily zone-2 plus three strength sessions. The protocol exists specifically to lower mitochondrial wear — and the biomarkers back it up.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Red light therapy. Room at 67°F.
Super Veggie — lentils, broccoli, cauliflower, shiitake.
Green Giant — EVOO, cocoa, berries, nuts.
Nutty Pudding. Final meal of the day.

protocol.training: 45-min morning cardio in zone 2, 3x/week compound strength (deadlift, squat, bench, rows), HRV-gated — skips if variability drops.
protocol.diet: Plant-forward, 1977 kcal/day, eating window 6:30am–11am. Every ingredient third-party tested. No alcohol, added sugar, or processed food.
protocol.sleep: Perfect sleep score obsession. 8.5h nightly average. Room at 67°F. No food 6h before bed, no light after sunset.
protocol.breathwork: Daily guided sessions. No extreme protocols — reports Wim Hof increases oxidative stress in his own labs.
protocol.supplements: 67 pills/day including rapamycin (6mg weekly), metformin, acarbose, NAC, collagen, creatine, taurine, lithium orotate, full-spectrum vitamins.
protocol.therapies: Red light bed (daily), NAD+ IV (weekly), HBOT (5x/week), plasma therapy (quarterly), full-body MRI (quarterly).

signature_practices: Plasma swap with his 17-year-old son (paused 2023 after flat results)
Nightly erection duration tracked as mitochondrial health marker
Entire Blueprint protocol open-sourced at blueprint.bryanjohnson.com
Publishes every biomarker publicly; invites critique

profiles.name: Peter Attia
profiles.tagline: The longevity doctor to Silicon Valley. Author of Outlive. Zone 2 evangelist.
profiles.bio: Stanford-trained surgeon who pivoted into longevity medicine, building a practice around the ultra-wealthy and publishing Outlive, the 2023 bestseller that turned Zone 2 cardio and VO2 max into household terms. Runs a popular podcast and clinical practice.

spend_breakdown.label: Diagnostics — DEXA, CGM, apoB, advanced panels
spend_breakdown.note: Quarterly comprehensive panels, CGM worn continuously

spend_breakdown.label: Supplements & rapamycin
spend_breakdown.note: Rapamycin, EPA, magnesium, vitamin D, creatine

spend_breakdown.label: Training & gym
spend_breakdown.note: Private coaching, Zone 2 bike, DEXA-calibrated strength work

spend_breakdown.label: Therapies — sauna, cold, red light
spend_breakdown.note: Home sauna and cold plunge, routine use

profiles.er_low_because: Protein-forward diet, sub-180bpm Zone 2 cardio as daily cornerstone, 4-5 strength sessions per week, aggressive sleep discipline. The weak link Attia has publicly acknowledged is stress management — he struggled with it enough to write a whole chapter in Outlive.

daily_routine.activity: Zone 2 on the bike — 45–60 min, nasal breathing.
Protein-heavy breakfast — eggs, Greek yogurt, berries.
Family dinner — protein, slow carbs, no alcohol most nights.

protocol.training: Zone 2 cardio 4x/week (sub-lactate threshold), compound strength 4x/week, VO2 max intervals 1x/week, rucking.
protocol.diet: 1g protein per lb bodyweight. Low alcohol, no seed oils, minimal added sugar. Occasional extended fasts (dropped after finding them counterproductive for muscle).
protocol.sleep: Non-negotiable 8h. Magnesium threonate, phosphatidylserine for stress nights. Screen curfew enforced.
protocol.breathwork: Nasal breathing during cardio. Limited meditation practice — publicly discusses it as a weak point.
protocol.supplements: Rapamycin (weekly), EPA/DHA, magnesium, vitamin D3+K2, creatine 5g/day, ashwagandha periodically.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge daily, sauna 4x/week (20 min @ 180°F), red light on recovery days.

signature_practices: The 'Centenarian Decathlon' — training for the 10 physical tasks you want at 95
Rucking as functional strength (pioneered the trend in longevity circles)
Public critic of resveratrol, NMN, and most hyped longevity supplements
Advocates for apoB screening over LDL

sources.title: The Peter Attia Drive podcast archive

profiles.name: Chris Hemsworth
profiles.tagline: Thor in production, surfer at rest. Owner of Centr, the $400/yr fitness app.
profiles.bio: Australian actor whose Thor transformations made hypertrophy-for-role a public fitness template. After learning in 2022 he carries two copies of the APOE4 gene (elevated Alzheimer's risk), he pivoted his protocol around brain health and published Limitless with Darren Aronofsky on Disney+.

spend_breakdown.label: Centr R&D and equipment
spend_breakdown.note: Home gym build, ongoing content production

profiles.er_low_because: Surf-forward outdoor lifestyle, movement-first recovery rather than sedentary rest, heavy daily protein load, APOE4-conscious diet (low seed oils, high omega-3). The APOE4 diagnosis functions as a kind of forced discipline — he treats brain health as non-negotiable.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Surf check or outdoor movement.
Strength session — 45 min, rotating split with Zocchi.
Breakfast — eggs, avocado, oats or sweet potato.
Surf or ocean swim with the kids.
Family dinner — protein + vegetables, no alcohol in prep.

protocol.training: High-volume hypertrophy during prep (2 sessions/day, 6–8 weeks out from role). Off-role: 45-min functional strength + surfing most days.
protocol.diet: 4000 kcal in bulk phases, 2500 in maintenance. Protein ~1g/lb. Low seed oil, Mediterranean-leaning since APOE4 diagnosis.
protocol.sleep: 8h minimum. Cool room, no screens past 9pm, meditation-assisted wind-down.
protocol.breathwork: Wim Hof-style cold breath work before plunges. Regular guided meditation post-APOE4 news.
protocol.supplements: Creatine, omega-3s, vitamin D, electrolytes. Intentionally minimal — no nootropics or PEDs (publicly stated).
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge daily, sauna 3–4x/week, ocean recovery after sessions.

signature_practices: Two-a-days for 6–8 weeks before each Thor shoot — 25,000 kcal weeks in bulk
APOE4-targeted brain protocol: exercise, Mediterranean diet, stress reduction
Centr app operationalises his regimen — $400/yr, 2M+ subscribers
Took a break from acting in 2023 specifically to reduce cognitive load

profiles.name: Andrew Huberman
profiles.tagline: The podcast that made sunlight a protocol. Morning light, cold plunge, zone 2.
profiles.bio: Stanford neuroscientist whose podcast turned obscure lab findings into mass-market morning routines — AG1, Athletic Greens, LMNT, and Momentous built businesses around his recommendations. Publicly criticised in 2024 for personal inconsistencies but protocol guidance remains influential.

spend_breakdown.label: Supplements (personal)
spend_breakdown.note: Momentous stack: magnesium, creatine, tongkat ali, rhodiola

spend_breakdown.label: Food quality
spend_breakdown.note: Beef, fish, eggs heavy; minimal processed

profiles.er_low_because: Morning sunlight discipline, 11-min weekly deliberate cold exposure, fasted cardio, low-carbohydrate protein-forward eating. Stress management is the wild card — the 2024 personal reporting suggested more disorder than the protocol implies.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. 10 min morning sunlight, no sunglasses.
Break fast — steak and eggs or similar.
Dinner — carbs timed to evening for sleep.
Wind down, dim light, no screens after 10pm.

protocol.training: Zone 2 cardio fasted, 3x/week strength (compound-heavy), rotating resistance and endurance blocks.
protocol.diet: Low-carb morning, higher carb at dinner (sleep-loading). Beef, fish, eggs. 16h fasts 2–3x/week.
protocol.sleep: 8h target. Controversial 'afternoon NSDR' practice — non-sleep deep rest.
protocol.breathwork: Cyclic sighing (two inhales, long exhale) for acute stress. Wim Hof occasional.
protocol.supplements: Momentous magnesium threonate, creatine, tongkat ali, AG1, rhodiola, omega-3, vitamin D.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge 11 min/week total (protocol), sauna 1–2x/week, red light morning.

signature_practices: Popularised 'morning sunlight in the eyes' as a circadian reset
The '11 minutes of weekly cold' heuristic
NSDR (non-sleep deep rest) naps as cognition tool
Heavy commercial entanglement with recommended supplements — disclosed in 2024

profiles.name: David Sinclair
profiles.tagline: NMN's loudest advocate. Lifespan author. The sirtuins guy.
profiles.bio: Harvard Medical School geneticist who turned his lab's work on sirtuins and NAD+ into a bestselling book (Lifespan, 2019) and a portfolio of biotech companies. Publicly claims his biological age is ~10 years below chronological — a claim that has drawn criticism for its marketing alignment.
profiles.er_low_because: One-meal-a-day eating, consistent HIIT, cold exposure, sub-1800 kcal intake. The supplement stack is more famous than evidenced — Sinclair himself has published caveats that his daily regimen is self-experimentation, not a recommendation.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Yogurt with NMN, resveratrol, metformin.
First real meal — plants-heavy, minimal sugar.

protocol.training: Short daily HIIT, walking, minimal structured weight training.
protocol.diet: Time-restricted feeding, one main meal per day. Plant-forward, low sugar, low red meat.
protocol.sleep: 7h target. Cold room, no late eating.
protocol.breathwork: Occasional box breathing. Not a core practice.
protocol.supplements: NMN 1g, resveratrol 1g (in yogurt for bioavailability), metformin 1g, TMG, vitamin D, K2, CoQ10, alpha-lipoic acid.
protocol.therapies: Cold exposure (shower or plunge) daily. Intentional heat periodically.

signature_practices: The NMN + resveratrol protocol — massively popularised, scientifically contested
One meal a day with calorie restriction
Publishes his own biological-age readings
Founded Life Biosciences, sold Sirtris to GSK for $720M

sources.title: Sinclair Lab at Harvard Medical School

profiles.name: Wim Hof
profiles.tagline: The Iceman. Cold exposure, controlled hyperventilation, 26 world records.
profiles.bio: Dutch extreme athlete who built a global practice around cold exposure and a proprietary breathing method, backed by Radboud University studies showing voluntary influence on the autonomic nervous system. The method is influential; the commercial ecosystem is contested.
profiles.er_low_because: Daily cold exposure, 30-minute morning breath-work, barefoot outdoor activity, minimal processed food, and no alcohol. Lives the protocol he teaches — the biomarkers from the 2014 Radboud study showed measurable autonomic control.

daily_routine.activity: Cold immersion — 3–5 min, river or bath.

protocol.training: Outdoor movement over gym work. Climbing, hiking, swimming in cold water.
protocol.diet: Two meals/day usually, mostly plant-based. Kefir, grains, occasional fish.
protocol.breathwork: Hof Method — 30–40 deep breaths, retention, repeat 3 rounds. Daily, morning.
protocol.therapies: Cold immersion daily. Heat contrast occasional.

signature_practices: Holds 26 Guinness records including longest ice bath (1h 52m)
Climbed Kilimanjaro shorts-and-shoes
Radboud 2014 study showed voluntary immune modulation
Pending lawsuits and family disputes around commercial empire

sources.title: Radboud University Medical Centre study, PNAS 2014

profiles.name: Jeff Bezos
profiles.tagline: Eight hours of sleep, famous transformation, and investor in Altos Labs.
profiles.bio: Amazon founder whose physical transformation in his early 50s became a tabloid narrative, and whose checkbook backs Altos Labs — a stealth longevity biotech reported to have raised $3B+. Treats sleep as a CEO competency.
profiles.er_low_because: Publicly committed to 8h of sleep as a priority since the 2010s, regular strength training with visible results, active lifestyle (helicopter, fishing, Blue Origin training). Stress load is enormous but buffered by control over schedule.

daily_routine.activity: Wake naturally — no alarm, prioritises sleep.
First meeting — no important decisions before this.
Family dinner, blocks calendar for it.

protocol.training: Strength-heavy 4–5x/week with private coach. Rowing, helicopter training.
protocol.diet: High-protein, quality sourcing. Breakfast of octopus publicly documented.
protocol.sleep: 8h non-negotiable. Publicly discussed as CEO competence.
protocol.supplements: Not publicly documented beyond standard concierge protocols.
protocol.therapies: Estimated — executive-level recovery protocols standard in this tier.

signature_practices: Sleep-first philosophy: 'I make a small number of high-quality decisions'
Two-pizza rule for meetings, noon lunch
Backed Altos Labs with reported $3B toward cellular reprogramming
Transformation narrative documented in tabloids and Vogue

sources.title: MIT Technology Review on Altos Labs, 2022

profiles.name: Mark Zuckerberg
profiles.tagline: MMA phase, cattle raising, 4000-calorie bulks. The surprise longevity arc of the decade.
profiles.bio: Meta CEO whose mid-30s pivot from pale hoodie into BJJ competitor became a cultural reset. Actively trains jiu-jitsu, surfs, and raises wagyu cattle in Hawaii — a physical curriculum that's clearly compensating for a decade of desk.
profiles.er_low_because: Intense daily martial-arts training, frequent outdoor time in Hawaii, heavy protein intake, surfing and foiling. Sleep reported at 7h — the weak link in what's otherwise a high-performance profile.

daily_routine.activity: Dinner with family — 4000 kcal day during bulks.

protocol.training: BJJ 5x/week, strength 4x/week, surf/foil 3x/week. Competition prep cycles.
protocol.diet: 4000 kcal in training blocks. Wagyu, eggs, rice. Own cattle operation.
protocol.breathwork: Cold immersion pre-training, standard pre-roll breath drills.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge, sauna at Kauai compound.

signature_practices: Competes in amateur BJJ tournaments
Raises macadamia-finished wagyu on Kauai
4000-calorie bulks to add mass for grappling
Daily foiling as active recovery

profiles.name: Elon Musk
profiles.tagline: Ozempic, Diet Coke, and a sleep deficit the size of a startup.
profiles.bio: CEO of four companies whose personal protocol is more case study than template — publicly credited GLP-1s (Wegovy/Mounjaro) for his 2022 weight drop, operates on reported 5–6 hours of sleep, and consumes large amounts of Diet Coke. High performance by workload, high ER debt by biology.

spend_breakdown.label: Private chef & food
spend_breakdown.note: Minimal time to eat, protein shakes

spend_breakdown.label: Recovery & sleep optimisation
spend_breakdown.note: Attempts to recover from reported 5–6h sleep

profiles.er_low_because: Chronic sleep deficit is the single biggest ER tax, and Musk is publicly on the wrong side of it (5–6h nightly). Heavy Diet Coke, irregular eating, multi-company stress load all reinforce the pattern. The GLP-1 weight drop improved one marker; the underlying mitochondrial load is high.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Diet Coke. Check replies on X.
First meeting. Skips breakfast most days.
Protein lunch if time permits — often skipped.

protocol.training: Irregular. Strength sessions sporadic. Martial arts claimed, rarely observed.
protocol.diet: Famously poor — Diet Coke, fast food, irregular meals. Improved since GLP-1 usage.
protocol.sleep: 5–6h reported; Musk has described Ambien and red wine to sleep.
protocol.supplements: GLP-1s publicly disclosed. Standard vitamins assumed.
protocol.therapies: MRIs after stress events; minimal ongoing recovery protocol.

signature_practices: Publicly credited Wegovy + fasting for 2022 weight drop
Sleeps on factory floors during production pushes
Tweeted about Ambien use — board cited it as a concern
Does not measure, does not track — inverse of Bryan Johnson

profiles.name: Jack Dorsey
profiles.tagline: One meal a day. Ice baths. Silent meditation retreats. The biohacking monk.
profiles.bio: Ex-Twitter CEO, current Block chairman, whose 22-hour fasts and Vipassana retreats made him the face of tech minimalism. Has backed off some claims publicly (fasting intensity) but remains one of the most disciplined practitioners in the tech cohort.
profiles.er_low_because: Time-restricted eating (one meal a day), daily cold exposure, sauna, meditation retreats. Trade-off is low caloric density — muscle mass is modest and reported energy is uneven on long fasts.

protocol.training: 7-min high-intensity daily. Long walks. Minimal weight training.
protocol.diet: One meal a day weekdays. Protein, vegetables, berries. Water-only fast Saturdays (dropped some weekends).
protocol.supplements: Minimal — salt, vitamin D, creatine, magnesium.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge and sauna stacked daily.

signature_practices: Silent Vipassana retreats — 10 days, no phone, no talking
7-minute workout over extended gym sessions
Popularised the OMAD (one meal a day) protocol in tech
Walks 5 miles to work

profiles.name: Tim Ferriss
profiles.tagline: The Slow-Carb diet. Ice baths before they were cool. The original biohacker.
profiles.bio: Author whose 4-Hour Body (2010) was the mass-market biohacking bible a decade before Huberman. Runs the most comprehensive interview archive in the space; publicly open about depression, substance experimentation, and psychedelic integration.

spend_breakdown.label: Recovery tech — red light, cold, sauna

profiles.er_low_because: Slow-carb diet is mitochondrial-friendly, regular cold exposure, kettlebell-heavy strength work, solid sleep hygiene. Psychedelic integration and therapy address the cognitive-stress side — a more honest approach than most in this cohort.

protocol.training: Kettlebell swings, kettlebell clean-and-press. AcroYoga. BJJ. Minimalist effective work.
protocol.diet: Slow-carb: protein + legumes + vegetables 6 days, unrestricted 'cheat day' on Saturday.
protocol.supplements: Magnesium, lithium orotate, vitamin D3+K2, creatine, HMB, selenium.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge 2–3x/week, sauna, red light, IV therapy periodically.

signature_practices: Cheat-day protocol — massive Saturday refeed
Early adopter of ketamine-assisted therapy, publicly disclosed
AG1 investor before it was AG1
Donated $100k to MAPS for MDMA-therapy trials

profiles.name: Peter Thiel
profiles.tagline: HGH whispers, parabiosis donations, a publicly-stated wish to live to 120.
profiles.bio: PayPal co-founder whose investments in anti-aging and seasteading, plus statements about his own HGH regimen, make him the longevity-movement's ideological patron. The actual protocol is opaque — most of what's 'known' is inference from reporting.
profiles.er_low_because: Hormonal optimisation and concierge medicine compensate for a reported high-stress, high-cognitive-load lifestyle. Less evidence of the daily habits (sleep, training volume, time in nature) that actually drive mitochondrial efficiency.

protocol.training: Light. Long walks. No public record of structured strength training.
protocol.diet: Low-carb, protein-heavy. Details not public.
protocol.supplements: HGH, testosterone optimisation reported. Stack private.
protocol.therapies: Access to the highest-tier concierge medicine network globally.

signature_practices: Donated to SENS, Methuselah Foundation, Halcyon Molecular
Publicly stated wish to live to 120
Invested in Unity Biotechnology, Gigagen
Considered Ambrosia-style young plasma infusions per reporting

profiles.name: Larry Ellison
profiles.tagline: Funded the Ellison Medical Foundation for $450M. Lives on Lanai. Plays competitive tennis at 80.
profiles.bio: Oracle founder, America's 5th-richest person, who at 80 still plays competitive tennis, sails, and owns 98% of the Hawaiian island Lanai (which he's turned into a longevity-tourism project). His foundation funded basic aging research for 15 years before transitioning to other causes.

spend_breakdown.label: Anti-aging research directed personally
spend_breakdown.note: Ongoing after Ellison Medical Foundation sunset

profiles.er_low_because: Outdoor lifestyle (tennis daily, sailing), access to the best medicine money buys, decades of demonstrated discipline around performance. Age is the rate-limiter — what's notable is how little age is showing up in function.

protocol.training: Daily tennis with coach. Sailing, light strength.
protocol.diet: Mediterranean-adjacent. Lanai-sourced where possible.
protocol.therapies: Tier-1 concierge medicine. Sensei Lanai wellness access.

signature_practices: Owns 98% of Lanai, built Sensei — a $2000/night wellness retreat
Ellison Medical Foundation — $450M into aging research
Still competes in yacht racing and tennis at 80
Diet of fruits, vegetables, and fish — publicly discussed in interviews

profiles.name: Naval Ravikant
profiles.tagline: Minimal supplements. Long walks. Twitter-as-philosophy. The $20k longevity stack.
profiles.bio: AngelList founder whose Twitter-native philosophy turned him into the unofficial guru of tech minimalism. Famously avoids aggressive supplementation, prioritising walking, reading, and sleep as the core protocol.
profiles.er_low_because: 'Walk 5 miles a day' is a deeply mitochondrial practice, even if he calls it something else. Minimal stress (post-AngelList operational phase), clean diet, and no ambient cortisol from overtraining or chronic caloric deficit.

protocol.training: Walking is the primary protocol. Yoga occasional. Rare gym sessions.
protocol.diet: Mediterranean-adjacent. Not aggressively calorie-restricted. Avoids alcohol lately.
protocol.breathwork: Meditation daily — Sam Harris Waking Up app publicly disclosed.
protocol.therapies: Occasional sauna. No aggressive recovery tech.

signature_practices: The 'walk 5 miles a day' heuristic
Famous for owning fewer than 100 possessions
Publicly advocates minimalism over biohacking
Daily meditation via Waking Up app

sources.title: The Almanack of Naval Ravikant — Magrathea, 2020

profiles.name: Jensen Huang
profiles.tagline: Works 14 hours a day, sleeps 6–8, and credits 'pain and suffering' for the NVIDIA legend.
profiles.bio: NVIDIA founder whose operational intensity is famously extreme — 14-hour days, daily emails to his entire org chart, and almost no public discussion of personal health protocols. Still in the chair at 61; whatever he's doing is keeping up with AI's moment.
profiles.er_low_because: 14-hour workdays and global travel are an ER tax, but Jensen is a rare example of sustained high output without visible burnout. Diet quality is reported good, sleep is reported 6–8h, and he reports joy in the work — which itself has measurable autonomic effects.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Coffee. Emails to entire org.

protocol.training: Light strength 3–4x/week. Walking.
protocol.diet: Protein-heavy, cultural foods. No reported restriction.
protocol.sleep: 6–8h, reported. 'I don't believe in work-life balance.'
protocol.therapies: Sauna. Nothing exotic.

signature_practices: Daily email to all direct reports (~60 people) summarising top priorities
Stanford commencement speech: 'I wish upon you ample doses of pain and suffering'
Signature black leather jacket, worn consistently for 20+ years
Rarely takes vacation; cited as both asset and risk

profiles.name: Sam Altman
profiles.tagline: $180M into Retro Biosciences. Personal stack: metformin, testosterone, testim creams.
profiles.bio: OpenAI CEO who put $180M of his own money into Retro Biosciences (a cellular-reprogramming longevity startup) and has publicly discussed a personal protocol that includes metformin and testosterone optimisation. Operationally high-stress; personally invested in fixing the stress.
profiles.er_low_because: Medical management compensates for what the lifestyle lacks. Short sleep during product cycles, high cognitive load, limited social time — the protocol is propping up a system under stress, not optimising one at rest.

daily_routine.activity: Dinner — typically late due to schedule.

protocol.training: Light strength 3x/week, walking daily.
protocol.diet: Keto-leaning, intermittent fasting. High protein.
protocol.sleep: 6–8h variable. Publicly discusses it as inconsistent.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge, sauna, reported IV drips periodically.

signature_practices: $180M of personal money into Retro Biosciences (cellular reprogramming)
Publicly discusses testosterone optimisation — rare for a Fortune tech CEO
Sam's shopping list meme: keto, metformin, cold plunge, Testim
Doomsday prepper — Big Sur property, gold, guns

sources.title: MIT Technology Review on Retro Biosciences, 2022

profiles.name: Beyoncé
profiles.tagline: 22-day plant-based resets. 90 minutes of choreo, daily. The cardiovascular engine of pop.
profiles.bio: Touring Beyoncé is arguably the hardest cardio job in entertainment — two-hour choreographed shows at elevation, night after night. Her 22-Day vegan reset with trainer Marco Borges became a 2015 book and nutrition program.

spend_breakdown.label: Dance coaches & rehearsal
spend_breakdown.note: Cardio load of choreo is the training

profiles.er_low_because: Few humans perform 90-minute choreographed cardio at altitude nightly — the work itself is elite zone-2/3 aerobic training. Plant-forward diet, disciplined sleep on tour, heavy post-show recovery.

daily_routine.activity: Wake (late on tour, earlier off). Plant-based breakfast.

protocol.training: Choreography is the cardio. Pilates, light strength, resistance bands 4–5x/week in prep.
protocol.diet: Plant-based 22-day resets. Off-reset: mostly plants, occasional fish.
protocol.sleep: Irregular on tour (7h target post-show), 8h+ on break.
protocol.breathwork: Vocal breath work daily. Meditation occasional.
protocol.supplements: Plant protein, B12, iron, vitamin D.
protocol.therapies: Cryotherapy, cupping (visible marks have been photographed), IV hydration.

signature_practices: 22 Days Nutrition — her plant-based reset program
Coachella '18 prep: 2 hours daily cardio + choreo, strict sugar elimination
Choreographer as training director, not trainer
Publicly discussed postpartum return pressure

profiles.name: Rihanna
profiles.tagline: Builds empires, not abs. Treats balance as a protocol.
profiles.bio: Pop star turned billion-dollar beauty and lingerie founder whose public position on 'diet culture' is near-opposite to Bryan Johnson — publicly refuses to obsess about weight, trained hard for Super Bowl 2023 but doesn't pretend it's her norm.
profiles.er_low_because: Happiness is mitochondrial — she's one of the few stars at this level who reports genuine enjoyment of food and rest. That said, sleep is often irregular (studio schedule), and training is cyclical rather than daily.

daily_routine.activity: Sleep — studio hours, late but consistent.

protocol.training: 3x/week maintenance, 6x/week during Super Bowl and movie prep.
protocol.diet: Caribbean-influenced, no restrictive phases publicly. 'I eat what I want.'
protocol.therapies: Spa and recovery standard, no exotic stack.

signature_practices: Publicly declined GLP-1 pressure narratives
Super Bowl '23 halftime prep: reported 6 days/week with Gunnar Peterson
Fenty Skin + Fenty Beauty are her daily health infrastructure
Back-to-back pregnancies with minimal post-partum pressure

profiles.name: Madonna
profiles.tagline: 40 years of macrobiotic discipline. Two hours of training, daily. Still on tour at 66.
profiles.bio: Pop icon whose longevity in performance — Celebration Tour at 65 after a near-fatal ICU stay in 2023 — is a direct function of 40 years of macrobiotic eating, ballet, and physical therapy infrastructure. Few musicians have been this systematically optimised for this long.
profiles.er_low_because: Four decades of macrobiotic eating, two hours of daily movement, and a full medical and movement-quality infrastructure. The 2023 ICU event was the exception that proves the rule — her recovery was faster than her age should have permitted.

protocol.training: Ballet, Pilates, resistance training. 2 hours daily baseline.
protocol.diet: Macrobiotic — brown rice, miso, vegetables, fish, no dairy/wheat/sugar.
protocol.therapies: Acupuncture, cupping, physical therapy daily.

signature_practices: Macrobiotic diet since 1984 — 40+ consecutive years
Celebration Tour at 65 after June 2023 ICU stay
Daily ballet regimen under Ana Garcia
Kabbalah practice integrated as stress protocol

profiles.name: Lenny Kravitz
profiles.tagline: Plant-based since the 90s. Gyms in leather pants. Celibate by choice.
profiles.bio: Rocker whose 2024 gym photos at 60 went viral, triggering the 'is-he-on-TRT' discourse. Publicly says no — credits 20 years of plant-based eating, yoga, and sobriety. A rare example of a rock star aging legibly.
profiles.er_low_because: Decades of plant-based eating, functional strength training, reported sobriety (no alcohol for 20+ years), celibacy as stated discipline, and low chronic stress. The rare male rocker at 60 who kept building rather than managing decline.

protocol.training: Heavy strength 5x/week. Compound lifts, visible size gains at 60.
protocol.diet: Plant-based 20+ years. High protein (pea, hemp, soy). Minimal processed.
protocol.supplements: Plant protein, creatine, B12, vitamin D.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge, sauna, massage.

signature_practices: Plant-based since early 90s — pre-movement
Publicly stated celibacy, framed as discipline
'Dodd Romero' gym photos at 60 went viral 2024
Owns a kitchen in every tour stop city for food control

profiles.name: Pharrell Williams
profiles.tagline: Looks 35, acts 22. The most public ageing-in-reverse story in music.
profiles.bio: Grammy producer and Louis Vuitton menswear director whose ageless appearance became a meme a decade ago — credits sunscreen, water, no red meat, and 'I have no idea what moisturiser does.' A case study in restraint over ritual.
profiles.er_low_because: Pescatarian diet, rigorous sun protection (mitochondrial skin health is real), low-stress demeanour. Travel load is high but rest is protected — the rare creative who publicly defends sleep.

protocol.training: Yoga, swimming, boxing — reported 4x/week.
protocol.diet: Pescatarian, low red meat, high water.
protocol.breathwork: Breathwork reported via Humanrace wellness content.
protocol.therapies: Dermatology-led skincare, IV drips occasionally.

signature_practices: Founded Humanrace — his skin and wellness line
Publicly credits sunscreen and water for ageless appearance
No red meat for 20+ years
Still DJs arena-scale events at 51 — cardio on demand

profiles.name: Bruce Springsteen
profiles.tagline: 3-hour arena shows at 75. The working-class blueprint for late-life cardio.
profiles.bio: Still delivering 3-hour E-Street Band shows at 75 — the most demanding cardio-endurance feat in rock, and evidence that consistent decades-long activity compounds. Public about depression and therapy; rare in the boomer cohort.
profiles.er_low_because: The show itself is training. Decades of consistent physical work on stage, active New Jersey farm life, and a therapy practice that addresses the stress piece most icons ignore. 75-year-old cardio that still delivers 3-hour shows is rare.

protocol.training: Strength 3–4x/week, farm activity, horseback. Show cardio on tour.
protocol.diet: Farm-to-table, protein-heavy, sober for years.
protocol.therapies: Physical therapy daily on tour. Cold therapy post-show.

signature_practices: Public about depression — 'Born to Run' autobiography
3-hour arena shows at 75 — unmatched in peer group
Sobriety for decades
Horseback and farm work as everyday conditioning

sources.title: Born to Run autobiography, Simon & Schuster, 2016

profiles.name: Jennifer Lopez
profiles.tagline: 'I don't drink, I don't smoke, I sleep.' Has said this so many times it's become a meme.
profiles.bio: Singer-actor-producer whose at-55 look became the early-2020s 'wellness discourse' pivot point. Credits discipline: no alcohol, no caffeine in recent years, strict sleep, and Tracy Anderson method.
profiles.er_low_because: Cornerstone sleep discipline (9h routinely), sobriety, daily Tracy Anderson movement, Mediterranean-leaning diet. The skincare infrastructure is arguably overengineered but the cellular inputs are clean.

protocol.training: Tracy Anderson method daily, 60–90 min. Dance cardio during tour prep.
protocol.diet: Mediterranean-adjacent. No alcohol, no caffeine for multi-year stretches.
protocol.sleep: 9h routinely — publicly cited as her #1 beauty secret.
protocol.therapies: Weekly facials, cryotherapy, IV therapy reported.

signature_practices: 9 hours of sleep as the non-negotiable
No alcohol, minimal caffeine
Tracy Anderson method — daily, 20+ years
JLo Beauty — her own R&D pipeline

profiles.name: Justin Bieber
profiles.tagline: Ramsay Hunt recovery. Public mental health arc. Still figuring out the protocol.
profiles.bio: Pop star whose 2022 Ramsay Hunt syndrome (facial paralysis) and public mental health struggles reset his protocol — pivot to sobriety (reported), therapy, church, and more structured training. Ongoing arc.
profiles.er_low_because: Chronic stress and sleep irregularity from touring schedule hit him hard (Ramsay Hunt is immune-mediated). The recovery arc — therapy, faith community, structured training — is exactly the right move but the deficit is still paying off.

protocol.training: 5-factor workouts with Pasternak, 45 min sessions.
protocol.diet: Low-inflammation, smoothie-heavy, protein-forward.
protocol.breathwork: Prayer as breath work. Therapy integration.
protocol.therapies: Cryo, IV therapy, PT post-Ramsay Hunt.

signature_practices: Ramsay Hunt syndrome 2022 — cancelled tour, prioritised recovery
Public faith practice (Hillsong historically, Judah Smith)
Sobriety reported since 2019
Pasternak's '5-factor' framework — 5 meals, 5 days, 5 exercises

profiles.name: Harry Styles
profiles.tagline: Pilates in Italy. Plant-forward. The low-key mitochondrial minimalist.
profiles.bio: Pop star whose tour shape is built on Pilates, running, and plant-forward eating rather than traditional strength. Public about meditation and therapy. A quiet counterpoint to the testosterone-forward male pop template.
profiles.er_low_because: Movement-first but not aggressive, genuine plant-forward diet, public commitment to therapy and meditation. Sleep is the variable — tour schedule is a known ER tax even at 31.

protocol.training: Pilates 4x/week. Running 3x/week. Minimal heavy strength.
protocol.diet: Pescatarian, Mediterranean-leaning. Italian aesthetic.
protocol.therapies: Regular massage, PT during tour.

signature_practices: Pilates evangelism (pre-trend) — part of the 2020s male Pilates wave
Italian summer aesthetic as wellness infrastructure
Publicly discusses therapy as core practice
Does not drink on tour (reported)

profiles.name: Jay-Z
profiles.tagline: 22 days vegan. Meditation practice. Built a billion-dollar stack around quiet.
profiles.bio: First hip-hop billionaire whose protocol is more about what he's removed than added — completed a 22-day vegan cleanse in 2013, practises meditation, and has publicly discussed therapy. Calm as competitive advantage.
profiles.er_low_because: Reduced alcohol (pivoted from heavy to occasional), committed to therapy and meditation, trains consistently. The work volume is extreme but the buffer — family, meditation, sobriety-adjacent — is operational.

protocol.training: Strength 4x/week, cardio 2x/week.
protocol.diet: Plant-forward, 22-day vegan cleanses periodic.
protocol.therapies: Reported — cryo, IV therapy.

signature_practices: 22 Days vegan cleanse — 2013 birthday challenge
Publicly credited therapy in 4:44 album
Daily meditation practice
Pivoted from nightly drinking to occasional — marriage and children framed

profiles.name: Christopher Nolan
profiles.tagline: No phone. No email. No chair on set. Walks 5 miles a day across locations.
profiles.bio: Filmmaker whose protocol is famously analog — no smartphone, limits email, refuses to sit on set, walks the production across locations. The discipline is as much cognitive protection as physical, and the output record (Oppenheimer, Tenet, Interstellar) is evidence.
profiles.er_low_because: Walks 5+ miles a day on set, minimal phone-induced stress (no smartphone), regular sleep, low inflammation diet by default. A masterclass in subtraction as protocol.

protocol.training: Walking is the primary training. Occasional strength.
protocol.diet: British plain — Earl Grey tea, toast, roast. Reported stable across decades.
protocol.therapies: None reported.

signature_practices: Does not own a smartphone
Refuses chair on set — stands and walks for 14+ hour days
No email during production
Shoots IMAX locations in sequence — built-in cardio

profiles.name: James Cameron
profiles.tagline: Vegan since 2012. Deep-sea diver. Built a longevity protocol around saving the planet.
profiles.bio: Filmmaker whose 2012 pivot to veganism (after making The Game Changers the following decade) was an ideological act that became a protocol. Still does physically punishing deep-sea and flight-based work at 70.
profiles.er_low_because: Vegan diet 12+ years, active deep-sea and aviation work (both high-intensity cardio-adjacent), strength training, and disciplined sleep. Vegan mitochondrial stack is often incomplete (B12, omega-3) but his is managed.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Vegan breakfast — oats, fruit, plant protein.

protocol.training: Strength 3x/week, diving, flying. Physical directing.
protocol.diet: Whole-food plant-based since 2012. Wife Suzy Amis is a key nutrition advocate.
protocol.therapies: Sauna, cold immersion reported.

signature_practices: Vegan since 2012 — publicly stated for climate and health
Deep-sea solo dive to Mariana Trench (2012)
Produced The Game Changers, Seaspiracy
Pilots helicopters and planes into his 70s

profiles.name: Quentin Tarantino
profiles.tagline: No smartphone. Writes by hand. The writing schedule is the protocol.
profiles.bio: Filmmaker whose creative output (10 films, books, TV) is built on a famously analog process — writes longhand in notebooks, owns a vintage movie theatre, refuses conventional Hollywood routines. Mitochondrially, reads as a moderate case with concentrated creative work buffering the lifestyle.
profiles.er_low_because: Reported irregular sleep during writing, heavy caffeine, moderate-to-high alcohol historically, foot-focused fetishising of traditional eating. Some creative joy buffer, but the protocol reads as a high-stress writer's life, not an optimised one.

daily_routine.activity: Films at New Beverly (owns the cinema).

protocol.training: Reported minimal — walking, occasional gym.
protocol.diet: American-style — red meat heavy, indifferent to trend.
protocol.therapies: None reported.

signature_practices: Writes longhand — no laptop, no typewriter
Owns and programmes the New Beverly Cinema in LA
Ten-film limit, publicly stated since the 90s
Private, non-celebrity wellness footprint

profiles.name: Hans Zimmer
profiles.tagline: Composes 14-hour days. Kept the cardio from playing synth. The studio is his gym.
profiles.bio: Film composer whose output (Dune, Interstellar, Inception, The Lion King) is delivered from a legendary multi-room studio in Santa Monica. Schedules are punishing, road tours in his 60s, and the protocol is less about training than about sustaining a creative life at industrial scale.
profiles.er_low_because: 14-hour creative days for decades, chronic under-sleep, mitigated by clear purpose and touring (which is physical). The real protocol is creative joy — not a biohack but a measurable stress buffer.

protocol.training: Touring requires cardio conditioning; off-tour minimal.
protocol.diet: Pragmatic — whatever gets delivered to the studio.
protocol.therapies: None reported.

signature_practices: Famously collaborative — hundreds of assistants over career
World Tour 2023 at 65 — synth/orchestra arena tour
Writes with synthesisers first, orchestrates later
Publicly values collaboration over solitary genius narrative

profiles.name: Marina Abramović
profiles.tagline: Sat for 736 hours in MoMA. The 'Abramović Method' treats stillness as training.
profiles.bio: Performance artist whose practice IS an extreme physical protocol — fasts, long stillness, extended silences, cutting work. Teaches the 'Abramović Method' which draws from Buddhist monastic practice. The protocol is discipline in its purest form.
profiles.er_low_because: Decades of fasting, meditation, and extreme-discipline practice have measurable autonomic consequences. Low caloric intake combined with strong purpose and physical practice — a template that matches some of the more durable monastic longevity datasets.

protocol.training: Endurance through stillness. Walking.
protocol.diet: Organic, often one meal a day. Fasts regularly.
protocol.breathwork: Core practice — daily meditation, pranayama, long silences.
protocol.therapies: None — the practice is the therapy.

signature_practices: 'The Artist is Present' — 736 hours of sitting, 2010
Method retreats: participants fast, don't speak, practice stillness
Student of Buddhism, Sufi practice integrated
Has performed physical cutting and endurance work into her 60s

sources.title: The Artist is Present — MoMA retrospective, 2010

profiles.name: Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson
profiles.tagline: 5am Iron Paradise. 6 meals. Cheat day cod and pancakes. The most documented routine in Hollywood.
profiles.bio: Former wrestler turned highest-paid actor whose training and diet are published in detail — his 'Iron Paradise' travelling gym and six-meals-a-day protocol have been the template for Hollywood transformations since the mid-2010s.
profiles.er_low_because: 5am training daily (2+ decades), controlled macros, structured sleep (7h+), and a support stack (trainer, chef, PT) that converts the lifestyle into sustainable output. Cheat day is the most famous meal in fitness — it's also a cortisol release valve.

protocol.training: 6 days/week. Body-part split. Intense but not max-effort. 60–90 min sessions.
protocol.diet: 5000+ kcal/day in prep, 6 meals. Cod, chicken, rice, eggs, vegetables. Sunday cheat day.
protocol.supplements: Creatine, whey, AG1, ZOA energy drink, standard stack.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge, compression, massage, PT daily.

signature_practices: Iron Paradise — travelling gym, set up within hours on location
Famous Sunday cheat day (pancakes, cod, cookies)
Trains at 4am regardless of timezone
Built multi-billion-dollar consumer empire (Teremana, ZOA, XFL) around the protocol

profiles.name: Hugh Jackman
profiles.tagline: 9 Wolverine transformations. Intermittent fasting adopter. The hardest-working musculature in Hollywood.
profiles.bio: Actor whose nine Wolverine transformations (2000–2024) are the longest-running physique-for-role case study in film. Credits intermittent fasting, progressive overload with Beau Baxter, and a well-documented 5,000-calorie bulk phase for Deadpool 3.
profiles.er_low_because: Consistent 25+ years of training through brutal shooting schedules, 16:8 intermittent fasting adopted early, reported 7+ hour sleep during prep, Broadway cardio maintains VO2 max. Skin-cancer discipline is part of the protocol.

daily_routine.activity: First meal — steak, eggs, sweet potato.

protocol.training: Hypertrophy blocks 5x/week in prep, 3x/week maintenance. Compound + isolation.
protocol.diet: 16:8 IF. 5000 kcal bulks, 2500 maintenance. High protein.
protocol.therapies: PT, massage, quarterly dermatology since 2013.

signature_practices: Nine Wolverine transformations — 2000 to 2024
Popularised IF in Hollywood prep
Broadway and film — cardio + hypertrophy hybrid
Skin cancer advocate — 6+ basal cell removals publicly disclosed

profiles.name: Henry Cavill
profiles.tagline: Strongman-style prep. 3-hour sessions. No shortcuts.
profiles.bio: British actor whose Superman and Witcher physiques are built on Dave Rienzi's strongman-adjacent method — heavy compound work, minimal HIIT, long sessions. Trains to the point of functional exhaustion.
profiles.er_low_because: Consistent heavy training with proper periodisation, disciplined sleep (documented 8h), real food in volume, low reported stress. The trade-off is very high volume which can burn out mitochondria — mitigated by Rienzi's recovery focus.

protocol.training: Compound heavy strength, 6 days/week in prep. Strongman adjacent. 3-hour sessions during Witcher training.
protocol.diet: 5000 kcal bulks. High protein, rice-heavy carbs. Clean sources.
protocol.therapies: PT, cryo, massage 3x/week.

signature_practices: Dave Rienzi method — powerlifting-forward for actor prep
3-hour training for Witcher Season 2 (documented)
Gaming as active rest — PC building is his hobby
Refused trendy supplements in interviews

profiles.name: Zac Efron
profiles.tagline: Baywatch tan. Down to Earth vegan phase. Publicly walked back extreme prep.
profiles.bio: Actor whose 2017 Baywatch transformation he later criticised — said the unsustainable leanness caused insomnia and depression. Pivoted to Earth-friendlier eating and discussed the mental cost of elite physique work.
profiles.er_low_because: After the Baywatch crash, he publicly pivoted to sustainable routines — plant-forward eating, outdoor activity, therapy. Sleep improved, stress load reduced. A rare public unwind of an unsustainable protocol.

protocol.training: Moderate strength 4x/week, hiking, surfing.
protocol.diet: Plant-forward more than vegan. Sustainable, not extreme.
protocol.therapies: Ongoing jaw rehabilitation, mental health therapy.

signature_practices: Down to Earth Netflix series — visited longevity sites including Sardinia Blue Zone
Publicly criticised 2017 Baywatch prep as damaging
2021 jaw surgery and public honesty about rumour cycle
Shift toward sustainable protocol as narrative

profiles.name: Arnold Schwarzenegger
profiles.tagline: Training since 1963. Pacemaker at 77. Still in the gym every morning.
profiles.bio: Seven-time Mr Olympia, actor, California governor, and the longest continuously training body in modern history — daily since age 15. Received a pacemaker in 2024; was back in the gym within days.
profiles.er_low_because: 62 consecutive years of daily training is cellular adaptation at a depth few humans have. Recent pivot to plant-forward eating improved inflammatory markers. The cardiac event is age, not protocol failure — recovery evidence backs the foundation.

protocol.training: Daily gym. Body-part split. Lower volume, same consistency.
protocol.diet: 80% plant-based since mid-2010s. Some chicken, fish.
protocol.therapies: Cardiac monitoring post-2024.

signature_practices: Daily gym at 77 — 62+ years continuous
Pacemaker received 2024, trained within days
Pivoted to 80% plant-based eating for climate + health
Runs one of the largest longevity newsletters (Arnold's Pump Club)

profiles.name: Sylvester Stallone
profiles.tagline: TRT advocate. Rocky-era training. Shoulder surgeries every 5 years.
profiles.bio: Actor whose Rambo and Rocky physiques were built on extreme caloric cuts and heavy lifting. Publicly uses testosterone replacement since the 2000s; the joint and cardiac wear is real and ongoing.
profiles.er_low_because: Four decades of extreme training plus historical heavy TRT/HGH protocols create an inflammation baseline that hormonal optimisation can buffer but not reverse. Still active at 78, which is evidence the foundation is solid — the markers would still be elevated.

protocol.training: Modified volume from 30s, compound focus. 4x/week.
protocol.diet: Protein-forward, moderate carb. 5–6 meals/day.
protocol.therapies: Shoulder surgeries periodic, PT ongoing.

signature_practices: Publicly discussed TRT use since 2007
Rocky IV training: reported 21,000 punches filmed
Wrote Sly autobiography detailing protocol evolution
Still producing and acting in late 70s

profiles.name: Jason Statham
profiles.tagline: Olympic-level diver turned actor. Training logs are public. The functional strength model.
profiles.bio: Ex-Olympic diving squad member whose training method (published regularly in Men's Health) emphasises functional movement, low weight high velocity, and high cardio baseline. One of the few actors whose protocol translates to actual athletic performance.
profiles.er_low_because: Olympic aquatic training foundation, decades of multi-modal conditioning (boxing, swimming, strength), no seed oils, near-zero alcohol, consistent sleep. Peak functional capacity at 57 is rare.

protocol.training: 6 days/week. Compound strength, velocity-based, kettlebell, running. Published 6-week programs.
protocol.diet: ~2000 kcal. Eggs, fish, vegetables, rice. Near-zero sugar. No alcohol during films.
protocol.therapies: Cold plunge, sauna, massage.

signature_practices: Olympic diving background — 12 years on British squad
Regular Men's Health cover with full training logs
Performs most of his own stunts in late 50s
Has maintained same weight (170 lbs) for 20+ years

sources.title: Logan Hood / Epoch Training published protocols

profiles.name: Ryan Reynolds
profiles.tagline: Don Saladino trainer. Deadpool prep public. Built his body into a brand.
profiles.bio: Actor whose three Deadpool transformations under Don Saladino are the most-copied superhero template in Hollywood. Public about anxiety and therapy — the protocol treats both sides of mitochondrial resilience.
profiles.er_low_because: Consistent Saladino-method training, cold plunge, clean diet, and active therapy for anxiety. The transparency about mental health is itself a protocol — chronic anxiety tanks ER, and he's addressing it.

protocol.training: Saladino 4-day split, compound + accessories. Mobility emphasis.
protocol.diet: Clean protein, vegetables, rice. Moderate carbs.
protocol.therapies: Therapy ongoing. Cold plunge, sauna.

signature_practices: Don Saladino 4-day program — publicly adopted and taught
Three Deadpool transformations, all documented
Publicly discussed anxiety and therapy
Cold plunge evangelist before it was cool

profiles.name: Chris Pratt
profiles.tagline: 60lb cut for Star-Lord. Daniel fast for faith. Evangelical wellness.
profiles.bio: Actor whose 60-pound transformation for Guardians of the Galaxy remains one of the most-discussed Hollywood body pivots. Combines Duffy Gaver-style strength work with a faith-aligned eating practice — the 21-day Daniel fast periodically.
profiles.er_low_because: Outdoor lifestyle (ranch, hunting), consistent training through career, community support via faith practice (social connection is measurably ER-positive). Fasting practice is moderate — Daniel fast is mostly plant-forward for 21 days.

protocol.training: Gaver-style compound strength. Ranch work as active cardio.
protocol.diet: High protein. Daniel fast (plant-only, no meat) 21 days biannually.
protocol.therapies: Basic recovery — sauna, ice.

signature_practices: 60lb cut for Guardians (2014) — Duffy Gaver six-month protocol
21-day Daniel fast — plant-based, religious observance
Outdoor-heavy life: hunting, fishing, ranch work
Public faith practice as wellness anchor

profiles.name: Terry Crews
profiles.tagline: Daily gym at 5am for 30+ years. 16:8 IF. Sobriety for 10+ years.
profiles.bio: Former NFL player turned actor whose training log is publicly available — 5am gym daily, 16:8 intermittent fasting since 2011, and sobriety from alcohol and porn addiction. Publicly shares protocol and recovery journey.
profiles.er_low_because: Daily training for 30+ years, 16:8 IF (consistent), sobriety from alcohol, active therapy practice, public faith and family structure. The rare profile where every variable is positive and sustained.

protocol.training: Daily 5am gym. Compound lifts, moderate volume.
protocol.diet: 16:8 IF since 2011. High protein, vegetables, rice.
protocol.therapies: Therapy ongoing post-addiction disclosure.

signature_practices: 5am gym — 30+ years, publicly documented daily
16:8 IF since 2011
Publicly disclosed porn and alcohol addiction; 10+ years sober
Men's Health regular contributor

profiles.name: Mark Wahlberg
profiles.tagline: 2:30am alarm. 4am training. Famous (or infamous) daily timeline.
profiles.bio: Actor whose 2018 Instagram-posted daily schedule (wake 2:30am, pray, gym 3:40am, cryo 7:30am) went viral and became both template and parody. Runs F45 gym chain and Municipal apparel.
profiles.er_low_because: Training consistency is elite, diet is clean, cold exposure daily. The 2:30am wake is controversial for mitochondrial health (chronotype mismatch) but if he's sleeping 7h (bedtime ~7:30pm) it's workable — just unconventional.

protocol.training: F45 + strength, 2x/day in prep. Daily compound lifting.
protocol.diet: 6 meals/day, 4500 kcal prep. Turkey, salmon, rice, vegetables.
protocol.therapies: Daily cryotherapy. Cold plunge. Massage.

signature_practices: 2018 viral daily schedule (2:30am wake)
Founded F45 investor, took it public
Founded Performance Inspired supplements
Catholic practice integrated into daily schedule

profiles.name: Tom Brady
profiles.tagline: The TB12 method. 23 NFL seasons. Pliability over hypertrophy.
profiles.bio: Seven-time Super Bowl winner whose TB12 method book (2017) codified his protocol: pliability training, 80/20 alkaline diet, resistance bands over heavy weights, hydration of 25 oz water per waking hour. Played at 45 — the most documented case of late-career optimisation in pro sport.
profiles.er_low_because: 23 years of elite conditioning, 80/20 alkaline diet, pliability-first training (low systemic inflammation vs. heavy lifting), extreme hydration, 9h sleep target, meditation. The TB12 method is explicitly mitochondrial: reduce inflammation, prolong the cell's peak.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Hydration load — 20 oz water with TB12 electrolytes.

protocol.training: Pliability resistance-band work as primary. Strength moderate, not heavy.
protocol.diet: 80% alkaline (vegetables, whole grains). 20% animal protein. No nightshades, no white sugar, no white flour, no alcohol.
protocol.supplements: TB12 plant protein, electrolytes, vitamin D, omega-3.
protocol.therapies: Pliability, IR sauna, ice baths, cryo, red light.

signature_practices: TB12 method — book, company, methodology
Plays in Super Bowls at 43, 44, 45
25 oz water per waking hour — documented
Retirement at 45 after 23 seasons

sources.title: Man in the Arena — ESPN+ documentary, 2021

profiles.name: LeBron James
profiles.tagline: Reported $1.5M/year. 23 NBA seasons. The gold standard of athletic longevity.
profiles.bio: NBA all-time leading scorer at 40, still performing at All-NBA level in his 23rd season. Reported to spend $1.5M annually on his body — cryotherapy, hyperbaric chamber, personal chef, trainer (Mike Mancias), biomechanist, masseur.
profiles.er_low_because: Single best-documented example of athletic longevity investment: daily multi-modal recovery, personal chef, trainer with 20+ year continuity, HBOT, cryo, IV therapy, plus the world's best medical team on call. Every single mitochondrial input is maximised.

protocol.training: In-season: daily practice + gym + court work. Offseason: 3-a-days for first month.
protocol.diet: ~4000 kcal in-season. No pork, minimal red meat, heavy vegetables, wild salmon, quinoa.
protocol.breathwork: Breath training with Mancias. Meditation with Wayne Dyer content.
protocol.supplements: Plant-forward. Reported no aggressive stack — focuses on food sources.
protocol.therapies: HBOT, cryo, IV hydration, normatec, float tanks, massage — multiple daily.

signature_practices: Reported $1.5M annual spend (confirmed 'over $1M' by LeBron himself)
23 NBA seasons at 40 — longest peak career in basketball history
Owns NBA all-time scoring record
Sleeps with blackout curtains, cold room, Eight Sleep mattress

profiles.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
profiles.tagline: Six naps a day. 3200 calories. Biological age reportedly 23.
profiles.bio: Footballer still performing at elite level at 40. Public protocol: six meals/day, six short naps/day, heavy recovery after every session, strict no-alcohol rule. Body-fat in the 7% range — one of the most measurement-driven footballers in history.
profiles.er_low_because: No alcohol, no sugar, six-naps-a-day sleep architecture, world-class medical infrastructure, and daily football-level cardio that targets mitochondrial biogenesis. Publicly cited biological age is ~23 — the markers support it.

daily_routine.activity: Wake. Hydration, chicken + ham + yogurt breakfast.
Sleep — 7h + six short naps totalling ~9h total.

protocol.training: Daily 2-a-days in season. Plyometrics, strength, technical. 6x/week match intensity.
protocol.diet: 6 meals/day, 3200 kcal. Chicken, fish, whole grains. No sugar, no alcohol ever.
protocol.therapies: Cryo, HBOT, compression, massage post-session.

signature_practices: Polyphasic sleep — 6 naps per day publicly documented
Zero alcohol ever
Reported biological age of 23
7% body fat at 40

profiles.name: Novak Djokovic
profiles.tagline: Gluten-free since 2010. 24 Grand Slams. The biology-first tennis player.
profiles.bio: Most Grand Slam wins in men's tennis whose 2010 pivot to a gluten-free (later largely plant-based) diet was credited in his book Serve to Win as the inflection point. Famous for meditation, breath work, and hyperbaric recovery.
profiles.er_low_because: Gluten-free, largely plant-based, daily yoga/meditation/breath work, HBOT, low alcohol, elite cardio from tennis itself. Publicly credits 'biological inputs' as his edge — it's not bravado, it's the training log.

protocol.training: Tennis 2x/day, yoga 6x/week, strength 3x/week, biomechanics with Marko Paniki.
protocol.diet: Gluten-free since 2010. Largely plant-based 2017+. Fish occasionally. Celery juice mornings.
protocol.breathwork: Daily meditation + specific breath protocols (he's an ambassador for Waking Up app).
protocol.supplements: Plant protein, vitamin D, celery juice.
protocol.therapies: HBOT, TheraSage (controversial bioelectric pod), red light, PEMF.

signature_practices: Book: Serve to Win (2013) — gluten-free protocol for elite tennis
24 Grand Slam singles titles
Daily meditation and yoga for 15+ years
Refuses COVID vaccination; used ivermectin during 2022 Australian Open drama

profiles.name: Serena Williams
profiles.tagline: 23 Grand Slams. Postpartum complications survived. Now investing in longevity and women's health.
profiles.bio: Tennis great whose 2017 postpartum complications (pulmonary embolism) became a global conversation about maternal care for Black women. Post-retirement focus: Serena Ventures, women's health investments, and public wellness arc through Aisha Beau fitness.
profiles.er_low_because: Decades of elite athletic foundation, pivoted post-retirement to sustainable training (no more periodisation stress), plant-forward diet, strong family + community support. Cardiovascular markers require ongoing management post-PE — but managed well.

protocol.training: Mixed tennis + strength, 4–5x/week post-retirement.
protocol.diet: Mostly plant-based, with protein emphasis.
protocol.therapies: Compression, lymphatic — ongoing post-PE.

signature_practices: Post-2017 PE advocacy for maternal health
Serena Ventures — invests in health/wellness and women founders
Plant-based shift post-2018
Still plays exhibition tennis through 2024

profiles.name: David Goggins
profiles.tagline: Zero dollars on biohacking. All miles and discipline. The anti-protocol protocol.
profiles.bio: Ex-Navy SEAL, ultra-endurance athlete, author of Can't Hurt Me and Never Finished. Famously spends almost no money on supplements, gear, or fancy recovery — his protocol is miles, discipline, and sleep.
profiles.er_low_because: Massive aerobic base from decades of ultra running (mitochondrial biogenesis is protein-stimulated by zone 2 volume). Clean diet, disciplined sleep despite brutal training. No alcohol. Stress is managed through output, not input.

protocol.training: Daily run (10–20 miles), daily lifting, pull-ups. Ultras quarterly.
protocol.diet: Simple — chicken, vegetables, rice. No alcohol.
protocol.therapies: None — stretching and ice.

signature_practices: Ran 4030 pull-ups in 17 hours (world record)
Recovered from childhood obesity to Navy SEAL
'Cookie Jar' mental practice
Spent sub-$20k/year on his own protocol — proof money isn't the input

profiles.name: Michael Phelps
profiles.tagline: 12,000 kcal/day in prime. Now an advocate for mental health over miles.
profiles.bio: Most decorated Olympian in history whose training in prime was 50+ miles of swimming a week at 12,000 calories per day. Retirement focus: mental health advocacy after publicly disclosed depression and suicidal ideation.
profiles.er_low_because: Elite aerobic foundation from 25+ years of swim training (mitochondrial biogenesis maximised). Post-retirement: therapy-centred, moderate training, family focus. The mental health arc itself is ER-protective.

protocol.training: 3–4x/week moderate. Swimming + gym.
protocol.diet: Normal adult — 2500 kcal. Not athlete-volume anymore.
protocol.therapies: Therapy ongoing; no exotic recovery.

signature_practices: 23 Olympic gold medals — most decorated Olympian ever
Ate 12,000 kcal/day in prime
Publicly discussed depression and suicidal ideation (Weight of Gold documentary)
Michael Phelps Foundation — water safety + mental health

profiles.name: Conor McGregor
profiles.tagline: Proper Twelve whiskey founder. Comeback body, training at altitude. The paradox athlete.
profiles.bio: Former UFC featherweight and lightweight champion whose last fights were derailed by a leg break, surgery, and substance controversies. Public return protocol: altitude training, BJJ, strict fight-camp diet — tempered by an ownership stake in a whiskey brand.
profiles.er_low_because: Fight-camp conditioning is elite, altitude training is mitochondrially maximal. Off-camp is where ER drops — public alcohol use (own brand), stress cycles, irregular sleep. The profile oscillates more than most.

protocol.training: Fight camp: 2–3 sessions/day. Off-camp: reduced.
protocol.diet: George Lockhart macros: protein, rice, vegetables. Weight-cut strict.
protocol.therapies: Cryo, ice bath, HBOT, PT ongoing.

signature_practices: Movement-based training with Ido Portal (early career)
UFC's first concurrent two-weight champion
Owns Proper No. Twelve whiskey brand — tension with fighter image
2021 leg break vs Poirier — still in long rehab
